name: Pin Matrix
explanation: An eccentric British-modular mono/paraphonic synth instrument. Three free-running oscillators (saw, triangle, pulse) feed a ring modulator that multiplies two of them for clangy, inharmonic metallic tones, then a diode-ladder resonant low-pass that self-oscillates, all shaped by a snappy trapezoid-style envelope. Osc Mix crossfades saw against pulse over a triangle bed; Ring Mod sweeps in the bell-like inharmonic content; Cutoff and Resonance sweep and ring the filter toward self-oscillation; Env Amount and Decay drive the trapezoid's bite; pitch tracks the played note and the signal stays bounded by soft saturation.
